reasoning: With only ~65 days remaining in 2025 relative to the 2025-10-26 knowledge cutoff, the timeline for Keir Starmer to resign or be removed is extremely tight. While he faces high unpopularity (-44% net approval), cabinet resignations (Rayner), and the Labour Together donation scandal, removing a sitting Prime Minister who commands a 172-seat majority just ~16 months after a general election is incredibly difficult. A formal leadership challenge requires 20% of Labour MPs (~82 MPs), and even if the threshold were met, the process leading to a resignation announcement would likely take time, further compressing the window before Parliament recesses for Christmas. Historically, prime ministers with massive fresh majorities do not resign over think tank donation scandals or early mid-term unpopularity. The market price of 9% on October 16 reflects some scandal-driven tail risk, but as time elapses with no imminent challenge formalized, this probability should decay slightly. Therefore, an estimate around 7% is reasonable.
